title: Limonium sogdianum
intro: Limonium sogdianum, also known as Sea Lavender, is a perennial plant species in the family Plumbaginaceae, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and grayish-green leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Limonium sogdianum include plumbagin, limonin, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sea Lavender is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to relieve congestion, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Limonium sogdianum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It is a type of saltbush native to Central Asia. The genus Limonium is commonly known as sea lavender, and it comprises over 170 species of plants. Limonium sogdianum is a relatively rare species found in specific regions of Central Asia. It is often associated with salty or alkaline environments.
